name: Police Data: Crime Reports

columns.name: Offense Category
columns.description: The most significant offense types, determined based on the severity of the offense or the frequency with which it occurs, belong in one of three categories: Crimes against Persons, Crimes against Property, and Crimes against Society. Crimes Against Persons, e.g., Murder, Rape, and Assault, are those offenses whose victims are always individuals. The object of Crimes Against Property, e.g., Robbery, Bribery, and Burglary, is to obtain money, property, or some other benefit. Crimes Against Society, e.g., Animal Cruelty, Drug Violations, Gambling, and Prostitution, represent society’s prohibition against engaging in certain types of activity.

columns.name: Year Reported
columns.description: The year the incident was reported to the Somerville Police Department. Separated from day and month to make sensitive incidents more anonymous.

columns.name: Offense Type
columns.description: Offense Types are used to categorize or organize the crimes committed throughout the United States. The use of standardized definitions is essential to the maintenance of uniform and consistent data. This practice ensures all criminal offenses of law, regardless of their different titles under state and local law or United States titles and statutes, are considered and appropriately counted.

Census blocks are numbered uniquely with a four-digit census block number from 0000 to 9999 within census tract code, which nests within state and county codes.

columns.name: Offense Code
columns.description: The three-digit data values are used to identify the offenses for submission by law enforcement agencies to the FBI.

description: This dataset contains crime reports from the City of Somerville Police Department's records management system from 2017 to present. Each data point represents an incident, which may involve multiple offenses (the most severe offense is provided here). Incidents deemed sensitive by enforcement agencies are included in the data set but are stripped of time or location information to protect the privacy of victims. columns.description: Includes requests related to the condition and upkeep of public infrastructure, such as sidewalks, roads, drains, and electrical systems, with a focus on repairs, upgrades, and safety hazards. Approximately 3% of requests are identified with this topic.

description: NOTE: Post-2018 crash data is available here: https://data.somervillema.gov/Public-Safety/Police-Data-Crashes/mtik-28va/about_data This data set tracks motor vehicle crashes within the City of Somerville from 1/1/2010 through 4/30/2018. This data was created by OSPCD in collaboration with the Somerville Police Department.

name: Police Data: Crashes

columns.name: Non-Motorist Location
columns.description: The location of the non-motorist with respect to the trafficway at the time of crash.  In the infrequent cases where multiple non-motorists are involved, only one value will be displayed.

columns.name: Cyclist Involvement (Non-Motorist)
columns.description: Indicates the number of cyclists involved in a crash, if any. A cyclist is one type of non-motorist which is defined as any person other than a motorist.

columns.name: Count No Apparent Injury
columns.description: The count of no apparent injuries resulting from a crash.  The injured party could be a driver, passenger or non-motorist involved in a crash.

columns.name: Count Suspected Minor Injury
columns.description: The count of suspected minor injuries resulting from a crash.  The injured party could be a driver, passenger or non-motorist involved in a crash.

columns.name: Speed Limit
columns.description: The posted/statutory speed limit for the motor vehicle at the time of the crash. The authorization may be indicated by the posted speed limit, blinking sign at construction zones, etc.

columns.name: First Harmful Event Location
columns.description: The first injury or damage-producing event that characterizes the crash type.

columns.name: Trafficway Description
columns.description: Indicates whether the trafficway for this vehicle is divided and whether it serves one-way or two-way traffic. A divided trafficway is one in which roadways for travel in opposite directions are physically separated by a median.

columns.name: Crash Location
columns.description: Address or intersection where the crash occurred.

columns.name: Latitude
columns.description: Latitude measures the distance north or south of the equator.  When coupled with longitude, a specific geographic location is identified.

columns.name: Count Unknown Injury
columns.description: The count of unknown injuries resulting from a crash.  The injured party could be a driver, passenger or non-motorist involved in a crash.

columns.name: Count Fatal Injury
columns.description: The count of fatal injuries resulting from a crash.  The injured party could be a driver, passenger or non-motorist involved in a crash.

columns.name: Roadway Intersection Type
columns.description: An intersection consists of two or more roadways that intersect at the same level.

columns.name: Traffic Control Device Type
columns.description: The type of traffic control device (TCD) applicable to this motor vehicle at the crash location.

columns.name: Road Contributing Circumstances
columns.description: Apparent environmental or roadway conditions which may have contributed to the crash.

columns.name: Weather Conditions
columns.description: The prevailing atmospheric conditions that existed at the time of the crash.

columns.name: Date and Time of Crash
columns.description: When the crash occurred.

columns.name: Crash Number
columns.description: A unique number representing each traffic crash.

columns.name: Block Code
columns.description: Block codes represent the census block where each incident occurred. Somerville is composed of approximately 700 census blocks.  Census blocks are numbered uniquely with a four-digit census block number from 0000 to 9999 within census tract code, which nests within state and county codes. These block codes are used to map incidents in the associated Tableau visualizations. Block codes are automatically assigned using the incident address and a small portion of incidents with limited address information may be mapped to a nearby block.

columns.name: Ward
columns.description: The Ward where the violation occurred.

columns.name: Longitude
columns.description: Longitude measures distance east or west of the prime meridian. When coupled with longitude, a specific geographic location is identified.

columns.name: Non-Motorist Condition

columns.name: Non-Motorist Action
columns.description: The action of the non-motorist immediately prior to the crash. In the infrequent cases where multiple non-motorists are involved, only one value will be displayed.

columns.name: Pedestrian Involvement (Non-Motorist)
columns.description: Indicates the number of pedestrians involved in a crash, if any. A pedestrian is one type of non-motorist which is defined as any person other than a motorist.

columns.name: Total Non-Motorists
columns.description: Indicates the total number of non-motorists involved in a crash.

columns.name: Count Possible Injury
columns.description: The count of possible injuries resulting from a crash.  The injured party could be a driver, passenger or non-motorist involved in a crash.

columns.name: Count Suspected Serious Injury
columns.description: The count of suspected serious injuries resulting from a crash.  The injured party could be a driver, passenger or non-motorist involved in a crash.

columns.name: First Harmful Event
columns.description: The first injury or damage-producing event that characterizes the crash type.

columns.name: Road Surface
columns.description: The roadway surface condition at the time and place of a crash.

columns.name: Police Shift
columns.description: The police work shift during which the crash occurred.

columns.name: Other Non-Motorist Involvement
columns.description: Indicates the number of non-motorists other than pedestrians or cyclists involved in a crash, if any. Examples include a skater, motorized scooter, wheelchair, etc.

columns.name: Count Not Reported Injury
columns.description: The count of not reported injuries resulting from a crash.  The injured party could be a driver, passenger or non-motorist involved in a crash.

columns.name: Hit and Run Flag
columns.description: Refers to cases where the vehicle or driver of the vehicle in transport is a contact vehicle in the crash and departs the scene without stopping to render aid or report the crash.

columns.name: Work Zone
columns.description: Indicating a crash that occurs in or related to a construction, maintenance, or utility work zone, identified by warning signs/signals/indicators, including those on transport devices (e.g. signs, flashing lights, channelizing devices, barriers, pavement markings, flagperson, warning signs and arrow boards mounted on the vehicles in a mobile maintenance activity), whether or not workers were actually present at the time of the crash.

columns.name: Manner of Collision
columns.description: The identification of the manner in which two motor vehicles in transport initially came together without regard to the direction of force. This data element refers only to crashes where the first harmful event involves a collision between two motor vehicles in transport.

name: Applications for Permits and Licenses

columns.name: Expiration Date
columns.description: The day the permit or license expires.

columns.name: Application Date
columns.description: The day the application was filed. This field is often blank because it is not a meaningful date for certain types of applications, such as annually renewing licenses. Issued permits and licenses without an Application Date may be viewed by Issue Date in the Tableau Visualization.

columns.name: Project Description or Business Name
columns.description: A description of the work and/or purpose for the application, submitted by the applicant. For some applications (usually applications for licenses or renewals), this field is the name of the business seeking the license.

columns.name: Department
columns.description: The department that is responsible for the overall review of this permit or license application.  Note that multiple departments conduct reviews on different kinds of permits and licenses. For instance, a Building Permit is an ISD permit, but other departments like Fire Prevention or Public Space and Urban Forestry may be reviewing and/or requesting application materials). The department listed here is the one with responsibility for overall review and issuance.

columns.name: File Number
columns.description: The unique identifier of the file this application falls under. The digits before the hyphen indicate the year the file was created. The digits after the hyphen are sequential for that year.  A single File may contain multiple Applications that are related, e.g. a building permit and an electrical permit for the same project. However, two Applications that have different File Numbers are not necessarily unrelated, as putting Applications under the same File Number is a manual process that does not always happen consistently.

columns.name: Status
columns.description: The current status of the application. This field is changed as applications move through the application process. Please note that the status in the data may not exactly match the status in the public Citizenserve viewer. They have been merged or re-classified for ease of use over the entire dataset. Where this dataset and the Citizenserve viewer disagree, the viewer should be taken as the source of truth.

columns.name: Application Subtype
columns.description: A more specific subtype of the application. Many types are subdivided with subtypes. See table in the Data Dictionary for a list of types and example subtypes.

columns.name: Application Type
columns.description: General type of application. See table in the Appendix of the Data Dictionary for a list of types and example subtypes.

columns.name: Application Number
columns.description: A (usually) unique number representing each application. The letters and numbers before the hyphen indicate the Application type and the year the application submitted. The number after the hyphen is the sequential application number for that type that year.

columns.name: Application ID
columns.description: The unique identifier of the dataset.

columns.name: Application Category
columns.description: The broad category of application: Licensing or Permitting. Licenses typically allow for the continual operation of a business, such as a restaurant, and may renew on a regular basis. Permits typically allow for specific work to be performed at a particular location, and may not be renewed.

columns.name: Active in 2025
columns.description: 1 if the permit or license was active at any point in 2025, 0 otherwise.

columns.name: Active in 2026
columns.description: 1 if the permit or license was active at any point in 2026, 0 otherwise.

columns.name: Active in 2020
columns.description: 1 if the permit or license was active at any point in 2020, 0 otherwise.

columns.name: Active in 2015

columns.name: Entertainment by Performers
columns.description: This license allows a restaurant to offer entertainment by bands, DJs, comedians, etc.

columns.name: Private Patio Seating
columns.description: This is an outdoor dining license on private property.

columns.name: Active in 2018
columns.description: 1 if the permit or license was active at any point in 2018, 0 otherwise.

columns.name: Close Date
columns.description: The day the permit or license was closed. If there is a closed date, the activity under the permit or license can no longer continue.

columns.name: Currently Active
columns.description: 1 if the permit or license is currently active, 0 otherwise.

columns.name: Active in 2024
columns.description: 1 if the permit or license was active at any point in 2024, 0 otherwise.

columns.name: Active in 2022
columns.description: 1 if the permit or license was active at any point in 2022, 0 otherwise.

columns.name: Application Latitude
columns.description: Longitude measures distance east or west of the prime meridian. When coupled with longitude, a specific geographic location is identified.

columns.name: Application Address
columns.description: The address the application pertains to, or where the activity is taking place. For example, this could be the address of the business for health licenses, or the address at which work is being done for permits.

columns.name: Issue Date
columns.description: The day the permit or license was issued. Until the permit or license is issued, this field is blank. Occasionally, a license renewal application will be approved, but the license itself will renew at a later date; in this case, the issue date may be in the future.

columns.name: Public Patio Entertainment Devices
columns.description: This license allows a restaurant to play music or operate televisions outdoors on private property.

columns.name: Public Patio Seating
columns.description: This is an outdoor dining license on public property and can include a parklet on the street, seating on the sidewalk, or both.

columns.name: Entertainment by Patrons
columns.description: This license allows a restaurant to offer dancing.

columns.name: Entertainment by Devices
columns.description: This license allows a restaurant to play music or operate televisions.

columns.name: Outdoor Dining
columns.description: A license for a restaurant to have seating in a public or private outdoor space; public outdoor dining licenses can include a parklet on the street, seating on the sidewalk, or both.  Somerville’s outdoor dining season runs from April 1st through November 30th.

columns.name: Application Precinct
columns.description: The Precinct where the property subject to the application is located. In Massachusetts, precincts are combined into wards, which are usually used to define local election districts. The number before the hyphen is the ward, so 1-2 is Precinct 2 in Ward 1.

columns.name: Application Ward
columns.description: The Ward where the property subject to the application is located.

columns.name: Application Block Code
columns.description: Block codes represent the census block where each incident occurred. Somerville is composed of approximately 700 census blocks.  Census blocks are numbered uniquely with a four-digit census block number from 0000 to 9999 within census tract code, which nests within state and county codes. These block codes are used to map locations in the associated Tableau visualizations. Block codes are automatically assigned using the Application Address.

columns.name: Application Longitude
columns.description: Longitude measures distance north or south of the equator. When coupled with longitude, a specific geographic location is identified.

columns.name: Assessor's Parcel Number
columns.description: An assessor's parcel number, or APN, is a number assigned to parcels of real property by the tax assessor of a particular jurisdiction for purposes of identification and record-keeping. The assigned number is unique within the particular jurisdiction. This number is also known as the Map-Block-Lot number.

columns.name: Amount Paid
columns.description: The amount of fees paid for this application to date.

columns.name: Application Amount
columns.description: The fee required to process this application.

columns.name: Business Days to Issue
columns.description: The number of business days between Application Date and Issue Date. A business day is defined as a non-weekend day that is not a federal holiday.

columns.description: Block codes represent the census block where each incident occurred. Somerville is composed of approximately 700 census blocks.  Census blocks are numbered uniquely with a four-digit census block number from 0000 to 9999 within census tract code, which nests within state and county codes. These block codes are used to map incidents in the associated Tableau visualizations. Block codes are automatically assigned using the incident address and a small portion of incidents with limited address information may be mapped to a nearby block.

description: This complete version of the dataset contains traffic citations issued in Somerville by Somerville police officers since 2017. Citations include both written warnings and those with a monetary fine. Every citation is composed of one or more violations. Each row in the dataset represents a violation. This data set should be refreshed daily with data appearing with a one-month delay (e.g. citations issued on 1/1 will appear on 2/1). If a daily update does not refresh, please email data@somervillem

name: Capital Investment Plan Projects FY16-26
provider: City of Somerville Capital Projects Committee
description: The City develops annually a Capital Investment Plan (CIP), which is a document that outlines capital projects for the next ten years, including infrastructure projects, building improvements, park redesigns, and equipment purchases. It also provides a proposed schedule and identifies financing options. This version was submitted to the Board of Aldermen in January of 2016 and will be updated in late 2016.

description: Every two years, the City of Somerville sends the Somerville Happiness Survey to a random sample of Somerville residents. The survey asks residents to rate their personal happiness, wellbeing, and satisfaction with City services. This combined dataset includes survey results from 2011 to 2025, including all 2025 questions with applicable responses from previous years. A data dictionary, exploratory visualization, and survey instruments are attached.

description: The City of Somerville completed the first-ever Participatory Budgeting cycle in 2023. In Participatory Budgeting, residents submit ideas for how to improve Somerville with one million dollars; volunteers score and select the 20 best ideas; and residents vote on the final ideas. This dataset describes the demographic characteristics of residents who submitted ideas. Residents could submit ideas through the Participatory Budgeting website. name: Participatory Budgeting Voting Results
description: The City of Somerville completed the first-ever Participatory Budgeting cycle in 2023. In Participatory Budgeting, residents submit ideas for how to improve Somerville with one million dollars; volunteers score and select the 20 best ideas; and residents vote on the final ideas. Voters could choose and rank up to 5 ideas out of the 20 selected final ideas. This dataset provides the number of votes in each rank slot for each of the final ideas.

description: The Out of School Time (OST) Steering Committee, a city-school collaborative group, initiated the Afterschool Needs Assessment Survey in order to better inform strategic planning for system-wide improvements in access to OST programming. The survey was developed and administered in partnership with SomerPromise, SomerStat, and Somerville Public Schools. We received responses from 742 families representing 1,046 PK-6th grade students.
